result.summary: Neon exposes one public HTTP API (API key auth), an MCP server (auth not fully pinned), and the Neon CLI (browser/API-key login).

neon_api_key.label: Neon API key
neon_api_key.setup: Go to the Neon Console API key settings and create a new API key. The docs note the token is returned only once, so copy it immediately and store it securely. You can manage keys in the [Neon API keys docs](https://neon.com/docs/manage/api-keys/) or from the console linked from the API reference.

neon_cli_login.label: Neon CLI login session
neon_cli_login.setup: Run `neon auth` (or use the Neon CLI login flow described in the [auth command docs](https://neon.com/docs/cli/auth)). The CLI can authenticate via browser or an API key and then stores the resulting session/credentials for CLI use.
